title: Bridge Essay: Naturalistic stimuli in touch research.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing, second-person neuroscience.

**Naturalistic stimuli in touch research.** — Curr Opin Neurobiol. PMID: 35714390. DOI: 10.1016/j.conb.2022.102570. Neural mechanisms of touch are typically studied in laboratory settings using robotic or other types of well-controlled devices. Such stimuli are very different from highly complex naturalistic human-to-human touch interactions. The lack of scientifically useful naturalistic stimuli hampers progress, particularly in social touch research.
